messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: This is a script using meshcore_py and meshcore-cli to periodically send out a message with time stamp and message sequence count to a receiving radio for range test purposes.
messages.content_html: This is a script using meshcore_py and meshcore-cli to periodically send out a message with time stamp and message sequence count to a receiving radio for range test purposes.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: The python script runs on a raspberry pi. It is kinda finicky because the pi's Bluetooth connection doesn't always work with the radios.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: putting this here for future reference: I can now reliably reconnect a device via bluetooth on the pi, but the steps are super jank. delete device from the pi desktop, bluetoothctl remove doesn't work reboot Heltec V3, remember the new PIN run bluttoothctl, scan on, then pair using mac address after successful pair, turn off and on the heltec v3 when it is back on, script using bluetooth should be able to send messages
messages.content_html:
putting this here for future reference: I can now reliably reconnect a device via bluetooth on the pi, but the steps are super jank.
  • delete device from the pi desktop, bluetoothctl remove doesn't work
  • reboot Heltec V3, remember the new PIN
  • run bluttoothctl, scan on, then pair using mac address
  • after successful pair, turn off and on the heltec v3
  • when it is back on, script using bluetooth should be able to send messages

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: I now have a script that call meshcore_py directly instead of going through meshcore-cli. You need a USB companion device connected to a computer running Linux (in my case, aPi4 running Pi OS). You need to install python and meshcore_py. here is an example on how to execute the script: python3 mcusbrangetest.py ttyACM0 "RAK 19026" 30
messages.content_html: I now have a script that call meshcore_py directly instead of going through meshcore-cli. You need a USB companion device connected to a computer running Linux (in my case, aPi4 running Pi OS). You need to install python and meshcore_py. here is an example on how to execute the script: python3 mcusbrangetest.py ttyACM0 "RAK 19026" 30
attachments_html:
mcusbrangetest.py
7.73 KB
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: I also used meshcore-cli to set the USB device (the sender) to hop through a repeater to the receiver. My goal was to test the range of the repeater. If I remember correctly, the command is something like this: meshcore-cli -s /dev/ttyACM0 change_path "RAK 19026" 3e
messages.content_html: I also used meshcore-cli to set the USB device (the sender) to hop through a repeater to the receiver. My goal was to test the range of the repeater. If I remember correctly, the command is something like this: meshcore-cli -s /dev/ttyACM0 change_path "RAK 19026" 3e

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: My script is similar to meshtastic's range test. Once started, it sends a direct message to a client every N seconds with time stamp and sequence number. Best would be to have this functionality built into the phone app so it is easier to run for everybody.
messages.content_html: My script is similar to meshtastic's range test. Once started, it sends a direct message to a client every N seconds with time stamp and sequence number. Best would be to have this functionality built into the phone app so it is easier to run for everybody.

My script is similar to meshtastic's range test. Once started, it sends a direct message to a client every N seconds with time stamp and sequence number. Best would be to have this functionality built into the phone app so it is easier to run for everybody.
